metadata.name: DataBindingBasics
metadata.description: Learn > hands-on data binding walkthrough. Builds a Counter screen from scratch to show @{binding} in three real contexts: value, visibility, event. Four sections + one closing troubleshooting block. ~10-min read. Companion to the concepts/data-binding essay — this one is the practical follow-up.

methods.name: onAppear
methods.description: Seed nextReadLinks from the module-scope NEXT_READ_ENTRIES catalog (two rows: next_concepts_data_binding -> /concepts/data-binding, next_first_screen -> /learn/first-screen) and stamp currentLanguage from StringManager.language. Each row's titleKey / descriptionKey is resolved through StringManager with the learn_data_binding_basics_ namespace prefix.

methods.name: onNavigate
methods.description: Client-side navigation via router.push(url). Destinations are the spec-mapped URLs enumerated in transitions: /, /concepts/data-binding, /learn/first-screen.

vars.name: nextReadLinks
vars.description: Two closing 'read next' cards pointing at /concepts/data-binding and /learn/first-screen. Seeded by onAppear from the NEXT_READ_ENTRIES static catalog and re-seeded by onToggleLanguage.

transitions.condition: url is a spec-mapped learn or concepts URL or /

notes: Fourth live entry under the Learn tab. Flipping LEARN_ENTRIES row 4 (data-binding) to 'live' in HomeViewModel finishes.

Practical pair to /concepts/data-binding — the essay there covers 'why', this one covers 'how'. Each section builds on the previous one, so the reader ends up with a Counter spec + layout + ViewModel that does all three binding kinds.

Four CodeBlocks total: (1) the skeleton spec + layout before any binding, (2) adding @{count} as a value binding, (3) adding @{statusVisibility} as a visibility binding, (4) adding @{onIncrement} / @{onDecrement} as event bindings — in that order.
